title: /Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/.claude 内容を表示せよ
summary_text: - **Primary fix (required):** In [`task_backup.py`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/task_backup.py) `def _reconcile_due_records` at **1448-1466**, change the SQL due filter so `prioritize_record_id` bypasses `gha_next_reconcile_at` backoff. Current behavior makes `record_explicit_completion` retry loop effectively single-shot after first `pending`. Keep retry loop in `record_explicit_completion` (**1027-1043**) unchanged; it will start working as intended once prioritized rows are selectable during backoff. - **Secondary fix (small, optional but useful):** In [`run-kernel-task-completion-backup.sh`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/scripts/local/run-kernel-task-completion-backup.sh), record path around **192-200**, append `--max-dispatch-batch 1` to `record_common_args` before `backup-record` call. This keeps explicit-run reconciliation focused on the just-recorded item and reduces interference from older backlog rows. - **Risk profile:** Bypassing backoff for prioritized rows increases reconcile polling only for explicit record flow (bounded by `EXPLICIT_RECONCILE_MAX_ATTEMPTS`, lines **36-37**). `--max-dispatch-batch 1` can slow backlog draining in explicit mode, but scan mode remains the backlog sweeper. Missing test gap: add one test in `test_task_backup.py` where first reconcile is `404` then success within explicit retry window; assert final status becomes `verified` (not stuck `pending`).
